implementation_summary: Created grantAdminImpl.ts (pure Deps-injected, zero context imports, AC-BOUND-1). Superset guard via isSuperset() (AC-GRANT-5). Retry loop (max 2 attempts, re-reads live adminPubkeys on retry). Idempotent already-admin short-circuit. Wired grantAdmin + getPendingRemovals useCallbacks into MarmotContext (type, useMemo value+deps, DEFAULT_MARMOT). AC-REMOVE-1/AC-REMOVE-2 satisfied by existing fireAutoCommit (no redundant guard added).

actioned: sev-4: test named 'superset guard' asserted the group_not_found path instead of the demotion_rejected branch (name-vs-assertion blind spot) — FIXED: exported isSuperset and added 5 direct predicate tests covering the drop case; split out a real group_not_found test
sev-3: isSuperset docstring overstated the guard as catching 'any caller-supplied set' — FIXED: tightened to refactor-safety-net framing
watch: sev-2: grant-admin-error-shape — exhausted-retry returns raw err.message while other paths use closed codes. Recorded in ownership-ledger as 'watch'; S4/S6 must map unrecognized error codes to generic makeAdminError copy.

stage2_codex.finding: Catch-based retry does not cover the realistic concurrent-grant case: marmot-ts commit() has no relay awareness and does not throw on a same-epoch conflict; both committers succeed locally and the fork resolves asynchronously on the receiving side, so one grant is silently clobbered while both callers see ok:true.
stage2_codex.verification: Confirmed authoritative via nostr-skills:marmot-researcher against ts-mls source (createCommit has no relay-awareness; ingest() drops the foreign epoch-N commit as unreadable; processCommit throws ValidationError on epoch mismatch). Spec 4.2 premise was falsified.
stage2_codex.resolution: Escalated to product owner (changes product behavior). Decision: accept last-writer-wins as a known limitation rather than build a stateChanged-driven re-grant subsystem (consistent with 2.4 / 4.6 honesty posture). AC-GRANT-7 amended; spec ## Amendments entry added; grantAdminImpl docstring corrected; concurrent-retry test reframed as the synchronous-throw path; MV-1 reframed to a degraded-path UX check.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The spec's 4.2 concurrency model rested on a falsified assumption about marmot-ts (that commit() throws synchronously on a same-epoch conflict). The catch-based retry implementing AC-GRANT-7 was correct against the spec but the spec was wrong about the protocol. It took a cross-vendor Codex review plus an authoritative marmot-researcher source dive to surface this — neither planner nor architect caught it because both reasoned from the spec's stated behavior. Lesson: protocol-behavior assumptions in a spec (especially for alpha libs like marmot-ts) should be validated against source BEFORE the AC is written, ideally at spec-validation time via a marmot-researcher consult, not discovered at review time after the implementation is built around the false premise.
